description: Hybrid UNION router: are the static bridge router (home + top-K W-neighbours) and the goal name-token router (home + top-m predicted foreign clusters) COMPLEMENTARY? The fusion controls e-0031 (global demand prior) and e-0032 (home-specific bridge prior W[home][.]) both mixed the structural prior INTO the predictor SCORE for one ranked list and failed; this tests the simplest untried combination -- the SET UNION of the two routers' chosen clusters: hybrid(a,b) = home + top-a static neighbours + top-b goal-predicted clusters. Same e-0030 setup (432 held-out cross-domain TEST theorems, train-only W/IDF/predictor, exact ranking-free complete-coverage CEILING metric). Pareto efficiency frontier (mean candidate pool -> max ceiling) over the full (a,b) grid vs the pure static and goal arms; head-to-head cheapest pool each family needs to reach each target ceiling.
motivation: e-0031/e-0032 score-fusion of structural priors into the goal predictor never helped, but score-fusion conflates the two signals. A set-union keeps them separate (static contributes the home cluster's globally heaviest bridge hubs; goal contributes the goal-specific specialized clusters), so it is the clean test of whether bridge structure adds any complete-coverage routing value beyond the goal's own name tokens.
formal_results: 432 held-out cross-domain TEST theorems, 27,705 train cross-domain, 92,594 clustered decls. Cheapest mean candidate pool to reach a complete-coverage ceiling, hybrid vs best pure arm: >=0.674 hybrid a=5,b=1 53,823 vs goal m=5 53,877 (ratio 0.999); >=0.70 hybrid 53,877 vs goal 53,877 (1.0); >=0.80/0.85/0.90 all tie goal m=10 72,322 (1.0); >=0.50/0.60 tie static (1.0). Hybrid/best-pure pool ratio is 0.999-1.0 at EVERY target. Hybrid points appear on the discrete combined Pareto frontier only as interpolation between coarse pure-arm breadth steps: e.g. hybrid a=1,b=1 = 0.322 ceiling at 24.5k pool sits BELOW the pure-goal-arm interpolation line (0.347 at that pool), i.e. dominated by the goal curve.
results_description: The set-union of the static bridge router and the goal name-token router reaches every complete-coverage target at essentially the same candidate pool as the better pure arm (ratio 0.999-1.0); the hybrid never beats either by a meaningful margin and its frontier points lie on or below the pure-goal curve's interpolation.
conclusions: The two routing signals are REDUNDANT, not complementary, under the prover-relevant complete-coverage metric. The goal name-token router already captures whatever the static W[home][.] bridge router would contribute at matched pool, so adding static bridge neighbours buys no complete-coverage (<=0.1% pool saving at any ceiling). This corroborates e-0032 with a cleaner mechanism (set-union, not score-fusion): the structural bridge prior carries no routing value beyond the goal's own name tokens. The goal name-token predictor is the dominant lever; the residual gap to the leaky oracle goal-router is predictor RANKING quality, not a missing structural signal. Still a premise-availability upper bound, not a measured prover success rate.
